title: Decadent Mexican Nuts Quesadilla
description: A creamy, cheese‑pulling quesadilla loaded with toasted nuts and a subtle nutmeg tadka, perfect for a protein‑rich indulgent snack.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a small skillet over medium heat and add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, crushed chili, and minced garlic, sautéing until fragrant and the seeds begin to pop.
Stir in the chopped nuts, cooking them for 3 minutes until lightly toasted and coated with the tadka mixture.
Remove the skillet from the heat and mix in the ground nutmeg, then set aside.
In a bowl combine the shredded cheddar and Monterey Jack cheeses, stirring to merge the flavors.
Add the toasted nut mixture to the cheese blend and stir until the nuts are evenly distributed.
Warm the flour tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds on each side to make them pliable.
Spread about one-third of the cheese and nut mixture onto one tortilla, leaving a small border around the edges.
Fold the second tortilla over the filling and press lightly to seal.

Melt the butter in the same skillet over medium heat, then place the filled quesadilla in the skillet, cooking for 2-3 minutes on each side until the tortillas are golden brown and the cheese is melted.
Remove from the skillet, let rest for a minute, then cut into wedges and serve hot.
